rationale: Author-of-record lens: the diff delivers exactly Bead 3's four prescribed steps against exactly the three plan key_file_paths (panelstate.go, panelstate_test.go, overview.md); the panel package is untouched, so the scope fence holds. Step 1: verdict() deletes the local matrix and delegates to panel.PanelGateDecision, keeping the enum + gateLabel and synthesizing the Allow reason locally with the plan's verbatim wording (both pinned TestPanelStateEntry_Verdict rows pass unedited — gate.go preserves the same reason substrings). Step 2: gatherPanelState sources bead facts via panel.ResolveGateFacts through a GateIO adapter (errBranchGone sentinel for IsRefNotFound, Worktree->absent so the dirty leg is inert); non-bead panels leave HeadSHA empty so staleness stays inert, byte-identical to the old IsBead guard. Step 3: 14-row delegation table + source-text literal check. Step 4: overview.md region accurate. shaPrefix removal is clean dead-code (only the deleted staleness branch used it). No unrequested scope. Build green, instruct tests green (z4ps excluded per BRIEF). The deliberate nil-Panel->Block is the correct fail-closed direction and now matches the enforced gate exactly.
findings: Scope fence exact: git show 25ad5ca9 touches only internal/instruct/panelstate.go, panelstate_test.go, and .mindspec/domains/workflow/overview.md — the three Bead 3 key_file_paths. internal/panel is not modified; no cmd/ leakage. No prescribed step missing, no extra scope.

Delegation correct: verdict() builds GateFacts{Reg:&reg, Res:r} and returns mapGateAction(PanelGateDecision(d).Action) for Warn/Block (relaying d.Message verbatim), synthesizing only the Allow reason. Verified in gate.go both Allow returns (fail-open, threshold-met) carry an empty Message and every Warn/Block branch sets a non-empty Message, so the empty-Message-on-Allow assumption is sound.

Pure-verdict / IO-in-gatherPanelState split honored: verdict() calls neither ResolveGateFacts nor git/fs; gatherPanelState does the one rev-parse per bead via ResolveGateFacts and stashes HeadSHA/MissingRef on the entry. This preserves instruct's read-only-snapshot behavior and keeps the fabricated-Result test rows working.

nil-Panel->Block deliberate change assessed: this state is reachable only via a Scan->Tally fs-race (panel.json deleted between the glob and Tally's re-stat), so the author's 'dead code' claim is slightly overstated — but Block is the correct fail-closed direction AND is byte-identical to what the enforced complete-gate returns for the same race (gate.go branch (2)). The old fail-open Allow was itself a drift from the gate; ratcheting it Block-ward is squarely in-scope for 'onto the single home,' not scope creep.

Minor observed behavior change (non-blocking, gate-aligned): the new bead path surfaces a directory-unreadable bead panel as a Block advisory entry (ResolveGateFacts synthesizes a PanelErr Result), whereas the old plain-Tally path silently continue'd. Fail-closed and matches the real gate, so acceptable and arguably more correct. Non-bead path still uses plain Tally and continue on error — the intended asymmetry.

GitErr state correctly unreachable from this path: the BranchSHAResolver adapter only returns errBranchGone (on !exists) or nil, so ResolveGateFacts never sets GitErr here — consistent with the table covering MissingRef but not GitErr, as the BRIEF/plan note.
